commonProblems.h2: Jeep Avenger Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Avenger's 1.2L PureTech and e-Hybrid engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing belt degradation and oil dilution the most reported concerns. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Avenger.

problems.group: Jeep Avenger 1.2 PureTech Timing Belt Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Avenger 1.2 PureTech Timing Belt Failure - Why EB2ADTD Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Avenger Longitude, Altitude, Summit (2023-present, EB2ADTD)
problems.rootCause: The wet timing belt runs in oil; degradation causes material to shed into the sump, blocking the oil pickup and starving critical components of lubrication.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Belt, tensioner, oil pump replacement + full flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before material sheds; not a fix once contamination occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt unit with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores reliability; upgraded belt kit reduces recurrence risk; best value for high-mileage Avenger

problems.recommendation: If your Avenger is under 50k miles and shows no oil pressure warnings, a preventative belt change with full system flush may extend life. Once oil contamination is confirmed, replacement with a rebuilt EB2ADTD featuring upgraded timing components is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2ADTD engine prices for your Jeep Avenger

problems.group: Jeep Avenger Engine Oil Dilution Problem
problems.h4: Jeep Avenger Engine Oil Dilution Problem - PureTech Fuel-in-Oil Issues
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent the engine reaching optimal temperature, causing unburnt fuel to wash down cylinder walls and dilute the oil, reducing lubrication and accelerating wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full oil flush, filter replacement, PCV valve check, ECU software update
repairOptions.longevity: May restore function if caught early; does not reverse bearing wear already caused

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt unit with revised oil separator, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; rebuilt units include improved separation to reduce dilution risk

problems.recommendation: For Avenger owners doing mostly short trips, regular oil changes with correct specification oil are essential. If sludge is confirmed via inspection, a rebuilt engine with revised oil management components offers the most reliable long-term fix.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Avenger PureTech engine replacement with oil dilution protection

problems.h4: Jeep Avenger e-Hybrid 48V System Fault - HN05/HN09 Battery & Calibration Issues
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Avenger e-Hybrid Altitude, Summit, North Face Edition (2023-present, HN05/HN09)
problems.rootCause: The 48V lithium-ion battery management system can develop faults, causing parasitic drain or communication errors with the ECU, triggering limp mode or no-start conditions.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset, ECU adaptation, battery management software flash
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable for software-related faults; does not fix hardware battery degradation

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt HN05/HN09 Hybrid Assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt hybrid assembly with verified 48V system, calibration included, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full hybrid function; specialist calibration ensures compatibility and warranty validity

problems.recommendation: If your e-Hybrid Avenger shows warning lights but still starts, a specialist diagnostic may resolve it with a software update. For confirmed battery or BMS hardware faults, replacement with a calibrated rebuilt hybrid assembly is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Enquire about HN05/HN09 Jeep Avenger e-Hybrid engine replacement

problems.group: Jeep Avenger Electric Motor Reduced Power
problems.h4: Jeep Avenger Electric Motor Reduced Power - ZKW Drive Unit Faults
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Avenger Electric Longitude, Altitude, Summit (2023-present, ZKW)
problems.rootCause: Faults in the inverter or thermal management system can trigger reduced power mode; true motor winding failure is rare but requires specialist high-voltage handling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic confirmation, inverter swap, high-voltage system recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if fault is isolated to power electronics; requires IMI-certified technician

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt ZKW Drive Unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt motor assembly with verified inverter compatibility, cooling loop check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full performance; rebuilt units include thermal management verification

problems.recommendation: Electric Avenger faults often stem from the inverter, not the motor itself. A specialist diagnostic can confirm the source. For confirmed motor faults, replacement with a rebuilt ZKW unit from a high-voltage qualified supplier is essential for safety and warranty compliance.
problems.cta: Enquire about ZKW Jeep Avenger electric motor replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Avenger
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with PureTech timing belt concerns, e-Hybrid calibration issues, or electric drive unit faults, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Jeep Avenger specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide supply & fit options. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Avenger Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Avenger and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Jeep Avenger Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Jeep Avenger's UK production timeline from 2023 to present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Avenger launched in the UK with the 1.2L PureTech petrol and e-Hybrid mild-hybrid, built on the PSA CMP platform shared with Peugeot 2008 and Vauxhall Mokka.
keyChanges: Jeep Avenger enters UK market as brand's first B-SUV/crossover
1.2L PureTech EB2ADTD petrol engine offered with manual or e-DCT auto
e-Hybrid variant introduced with HN05 code and 48V belt-starter generator
Fully electric variant launched with ZKW motor and 54kWh battery
mainEngines: ZKW - Electric drive unit (Electric Longitude / Altitude / Summit)
knownFor: Strong initial demand for compact electric SUV segment
Early reports of PureTech timing belt concerns from owner forums
e-Hybrid calibration issues reported on first-year units
Electric variant praised for urban range but limited rapid charging speed
years.cta: Get quotes for 2023 Jeep Avenger replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Avenger EB2ADTD timing belt • Jeep Avenger e-Hybrid no-start • Jeep Avenger electric motor reduced power
enquiries: Jeep Avenger electric motor reduced power

years.description: Mid-cycle updates brought revised e-Hybrid software (HN09 code) and the limited North Face Edition, while addressing early PureTech concerns with updated service procedures.
keyChanges: Revised e-Hybrid engine code HN09 introduced with updated ECU calibration
North Face Edition limited trim launched with exclusive styling and equipment
Updated service bulletins for PureTech timing belt inspection intervals
Electric variant received minor software improvements for thermal management
mainEngines: HN09 - 1.2L e-Hybrid revised code (Altitude / Summit / North Face)
ZKW - Electric drive unit (all electric trims, continued)
knownFor: HN09 code reduces calibration mismatch issues post-engine swap
North Face Edition commands premium pricing but uses standard powertrain
PureTech oil dilution concerns persist for short-journey drivers
Electric variant remains popular for urban fleets and company car users

items.question: How much does a Jeep Avenger engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine prices for the Jeep Avenger range from £1,400 for a basic 1.2L PureTech unit to £8,500 for an electric ZKW drive unit, depending on engine code, condition and whether supply & fit is included.
keyPoints: Petrol EB2ADTD rebuilt: £1,400-£3,200 supply only
e-Hybrid HN05/HN09 rebuilt: £1,800-£3,800 supply only
Electric ZKW motor rebuilt: £4,500-£8,500 supply only
Supply & fit adds £450-£950 depending on garage location
All prices include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty

items.question: What engine code does my Jeep Avenger have?
items.answer: Most Jeep Avengers use EB2ADTD (petrol), HN05/HN09 (e-Hybrid) or ZKW (electric). Check your V5C logbook, engine bay sticker, or enter your registration above for instant verification via DVLA.
keyPoints: EB2ADTD: 1.2L PureTech petrol, 100hp, manual or e-DCT auto
HN05/HN09: 1.2L e-Hybrid mild-hybrid, 110hp combined output
ZKW: Electric drive unit, 156hp, 54kWh battery
Engine code is critical for compatibility and warranty validation
Free VIN/engine code check available before ordering
items.cta: Verify your Jeep Avenger engine code instantly

items.question: Is the Jeep Avenger 1.2 PureTech engine reliable?
items.answer: The EB2ADTD PureTech has known timing belt and oil dilution concerns on short-journey use, but rebuilt units with upgraded timing components and revised oil management significantly improve long-term reliability.
keyPoints: Timing belt runs in oil; degradation can cause contamination from 40k miles
Oil dilution risk on frequent short trips without full warm-up cycles
Rebuilt engines include upgraded belt kits and improved oil separators
Regular oil changes with correct specification reduce failure risk
Early detection via oil pressure monitoring can prevent catastrophic damage

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Jeep Avenger engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines undergo full disassembly with all wear items replaced and upgraded components for known failures; reconditioned units are cleaned and inspected with only failed parts replaced, offering a lower-cost option for sound cores.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: comprehensive overhaul, upgraded timing components, dyno-tested
Reconditioned: targeted repair, faster turnaround, lower upfront cost
Both include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Rebuilt recommended for high-mileage or failure-prone EB2ADTD units
Reconditioned suitable for lower-mileage Avengers with single known faults
